user_query:
I’m putting together a prototype “data bundle” for our Rex Rivers fan club site. Please use only the local files under input/ and deliver a reproducible bundle with aggregated, cross-checked data and a short summary. Inputs:
- input/data/artist.yaml
- input/data/albums.json
- input/data/tracks.csv
- input/data/fan_posts.jsonl
- all interview HTML files under input/assets/web/ (discover them by inspecting that directory; don’t hardcode names)

Deliverables:
1) output/content.json – a single JSON document with these top-level keys:
   - artist: { name: string, hometown: string }
   - albums: array of { album_id, title, year, track_count } copied from albums.json
   - tracks: array built from tracks.csv as { title, album_id, duration, mentioned_in_fan_posts }, where mentioned_in_fan_posts is the integer count of case-insensitive mentions of the exact track title in fan_posts.jsonl “text” fields.
   - interviews: array with one object per HTML interview discovered under input/assets/web/. For each interview, extract:
     • title: the article H1 text
     • date: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) from the <time> element’s datetime attribute
     • interviewer: the person named in the byline (strip the leading “By ”)
     • quotes: all <blockquote> texts, as an array of strings
     • mentions: { albums: [album_id], tracks: [track_title] } where albums and tracks are those whose titles appear in any quote (case-insensitive exact title match against data/albums.json and data/tracks.csv). Use album_id values for albums.
2) output/report/mismatches.csv – a CSV report with headers album_id,expected_track_count,actual_track_count listing any albums where the number of tracks in tracks.csv does not equal the album’s track_count in albums.json.
3) output/summary.md – a short human-readable summary that includes: total albums, total tracks, total interviews processed, and the number of mismatched albums found.

Notes/constraints:
- Inspect the input/assets/web directory to find every *.html interview to include.
- Read and parse all listed local files; do not access the Internet.
- Use case-insensitive matching when counting track mentions in fan posts and when tagging interview quote mentions. Count each post toward a track at most once per post.
- The outputs must be deterministic so I can delete output/ and rerun to get the same results.

Output paths must be exactly:
- output/content.json
- output/report/mismatches.csv
- output/summary.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
